Richard F. Rescho, ESq. of the Law Offices of CHRISTOPHER GRELL 510/832-2980 for Plaintiff and opposing party on defendant SABERGAGEN HOLDINGS' motion to quash service of summons for lack of personal jurisdiction
* Counsel represented that both parties reached an agreement to take this matter off calaendar. Court informed counsel that this must be done by the Moving Party. MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Jennifer A. Cormier, Esq. of WALSWORTH, FRANKLIN, et al. 781-7072 specially appearing for defendant SABERHAGEN HOLDINGS moving to quash service of summons
Richard F. Rescho, Esq. of the Law Offices of CHRISTOPHER E. GRELL 510/832-2980 for Plaintiff and opposing party
* * * Heard at the same time as Line No. 9 (321327 McKennett vs. Asbestos Defendants).
Argued and the Court continued this moton to JULY 17, 2002 to allow the deposition of Ms. MADSEN to go forward. Ms. Madsen is located in Seattle, Washington and all Plaintiffs counsel is to travel to take this deposition. Deposition is limited to jurisdiction issue. Plaintiffs required to coordinate this deposition so that it will be a single session. Supplemental opposition will be due on July 8th; any supplemental reply thereto by July 12th. All briefs to be served personally or by FAX transmission, close of business. Plaintiff to prepare order consistent with this ruling. (JJM/rc) LEE BATARA, CSR 3759
